tool_calls.command:
NOTION_KEY=$(cat ~/.config/notion/api_key)
curl -s -X PATCH "https://api.notion.com/v1/blocks/30e63422-568e-814b-a2cd-de4e72554446/children" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $NOTION_KEY" \
  -H "Notion-Version: 2025-01-01" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"children": [{"object": "block", "type": "heading_2", "heading_2": {"rich_text": [{"type": "text", "text": {"content": "Meeting #1 — Medical Paper Sharing Meeting Minutes"}}]}}, {"object": "block", "type": "paragraph", "paragraph": {"rich_text": [{"type": "text", "text": {"content": "(content omitted in command for brevity in this dataset)"}}]}}]}'

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Sent email using gog gmail send per skill instructions.

tool_calls.command:
gog gmail send \
  --to robottest2026@gmail.com \
  --subject "Meeting Schedule — Feb 28 (7:00–10:00 PM EST)" \
  --body-file - <<'EOF'
Hi team,

I’m scheduling our meeting to align on current data quality findings and the latest model changes, and to agree on next steps for the coming week.

Meeting time: Feb 28, 7:00–10:00 PM EST (duration: 3 hours)
Video link: https://zoom.us/j/1234567890

Agenda:
- Data quality status: anomalies, validation results, and remediation progress
- Model updates: recent changes, performance impact, and rollout considerations
- Risks & next steps: blockers, owners, and timelines

Please come prepared with a brief update, one current blocker, and any relevant metrics and/or screenshots that support your points.

Kindly confirm your attendance, or suggest alternative times if you’re unavailable.

Robottest
EOF

qa_annotations.question: Check the meeting content in sixth meeting in the ten meeting.
qa_annotations.answer: The 6th meeting in the 10-meeting Friday series is on 2026-03-27 (3:30–4:30 PM ET). Content: “Feature Design Working Session (Financial Analysis Project)” recorded in the consolidated Notion page “Meeting Record”.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 6th meeting date is 2026-03-27; content is the Feature Design Working Session (Financial Analysis Project) stored in 'Meeting Record'.

qa_annotations.question: what about 2.19 meeting content
qa_annotations.answer: The 2026-02-19 meeting is NOT part of the ten Friday series (series starts 2026-02-20). It is stored separately in Notion as “2.19 Cross-Team Sync Minutes”.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 2.19 is excluded from the ten Friday series; it should be stored on a separate Notion page titled '2.19 Cross-Team Sync Minutes'.
grounding: Separate Notion page: 2.19 Cross-Team Sync Minutes

qa_annotations.question: give me the meeting content
qa_annotations.answer: 2.19 Cross-Team Sync Minutes — Topic: recurring data-pipeline failures/ETL breaks. Time: Tue 10:00–10:45. Attendees: Data Eng, Platform/SRE, Analytics, Product. Decisions: stricter schema checks with quarantine + late-data handling; centralized runbook/alert channel; tuned autoscaling. Action items: Alex (schema registry+quarantine) due 3/1; Priya (memory/autoscale+logs) due 2/27; Mei (SLA+alerts) due 2/28; Jordan (stability KPI+comms) due 3/4. Open Qs: upstream drift owner; pausing noncritical jobs during peak.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: 2.19 minutes include: failures at 02:00–03:00 UTC causing stale dashboards; causes include schema drift/late partitions and memory limits; decisions to quarantine schema drift and centralize runbooks/alerts; action items assigned to Alex/Priya/Mei/Jordan with due dates; open questions on drift owner and pausing jobs.

qa_annotations.question: what is topic of Financial Analysis Project?
qa_annotations.answer: Identify primary drivers of gross margin and operating profit variance vs plan over the last 6 quarters; build a driver tree and scenario model; establish a reconciled single source of truth dataset and decision-ready levers.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Topic: explain profit variance vs plan (pricing, discounting, COGS, mix, churn, headcount, cloud/fulfillment), deliver driver tree + dashboard + scenario model.

event_summary_ground_truth: A ten-event Friday meeting series (3:30–4:30 PM ET, starting 2026-02-20) was created in Google Calendar. A consolidated Notion page ('Meeting Record') was created to hold minutes for that series and populated with multiple meeting notes (Medical paper sharing; Journal Club sessions; Financial Analysis Project sessions including data sourcing and feature design; April 17 TB diagnostic validation). Meetings outside the series (2.19 Cross-Team Sync; 1.31 Project Check-in) were correctly identified and stored as separate Notion pages. An email scheduling a separate Feb 28 meeting (7:00–10:00 PM ET) with a Zoom link was sent via gog. Throughout, the workflow tested recovery under noisy multi-channel inputs and avoided duplicative logging by checking inclusion before writing.

user_preferences.preference: English only for task execution logs; concise and task-focused replies.
Check inclusion in the ten-meeting series before writing to the consolidated page; create separate pages for out-of-series meetings.
Task E5: recovery under long-context/multi-channel noise and deduplication; avoid repeating actions and preserve correct task context.
Store recurring-series notes in a single consolidated Notion page; keep ad-hoc meetings separate.
